/01 ABOUT ME

ABOUT ME

I'm Jitendra T. Sahu, a final-year B.Tech (CSE) student and aspiring cybersecurity professional from Nagpur, Maharashtra, India. I specialize in web application security, bug bounty hunting, and responsible vulnerability disclosure.

With hands-on experience identifying authentication bypasses, business logic flaws, IDOR vulnerabilities, account takeover chains, and token manipulation across real production environments — recognized in 3 Security Researcher Halls of Fame and earned a $100 bounty for a critical finding.

Built open-source security tools on GitHub, completed a 3-month VAPT internship at Phoenix Cyber Secure, and published detailed writeups on LinkedIn covering research methodology and findings.

Seeking: Penetration Testing · VAPT · Offensive Security · Application Security roles.

/06 PROJECTS

FEATURED PROJECTS

// 01 — OFFENSIVE TOOL
XSS SCANNER
Python-based automated Cross-Site Scripting vulnerability scanner. Detects reflected and stored XSS across web application parameters using a recon-first methodology to reduce false positives and maximize coverage on large attack surfaces.
PYTHONXSSAUTOMATIONRECON
VIEW ON GITHUB →
// 02 — RECON FRAMEWORK
RECONMASTER
Practical Web Reconnaissance Workflow — structured methodology tool covering subdomain enumeration, port scanning, endpoint discovery, parameter fuzzing, and JS file analysis. Built for bug bounty hunters and penetration testers.
PYTHONRECONOSINTBASH
VIEW ON GITHUB →

/10 WRITEUPS

SECURITY WRITEUPS

I document bug bounty findings, penetration testing methodologies, and vulnerability research as detailed technical writeups. Each covers the full discovery process — from initial recon, to vulnerability identification, exploitation PoC, and responsible disclosure timeline.

Topics: authentication bypasses, business logic exploitation, IDOR chains, account takeover flows, and recon workflows. Published on LinkedIn with step-by-step technical breakdowns.
